input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image showing seven CAD views (each boxed in black) of our vehicle design. The top, big view shows a close-up view of the design. The six smaller views on the bottom of the image show different complete views of the CAD of the vehicle and are provided for context. Note that the close-up view orientation matches one of the six complete view orientations. The close-up view may also have some components hidden (with respect to the corresponding complete view) for visualization of specific components. Looking at the close-up view, is/are any part of the the nosecone and body panels visible in the close-up view? Answer simply with yes or no.

retrieved_context:
RULE: in.2.3
            TITLE: visible access

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.2.3: Visible Access
IN.2.3 Visible Access  
All items on the Technical Inspection Form must be clearly visible to the technical inspectors 
without using instruments such as endoscopes or mirrors.  
Methods to provide visible access include but are not limited to removable body panels, access 
panels, and other components

RULE: ic.5.4.6
            TITLE: the sight tube and fuel level line must be clearly visible to two individuals (one to fill the tank,

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.5.4.6: The sight tube and fuel level line must be clearly visible to two individuals (one to fill the tank,
IC.5.4.6 The sight tube and fuel level line must be clearly visible to two individuals (one to fill the tank, 
the other to visually verify fill) without the need of assistance (artificial lighting, magnifiers, 
etc) or the need to remove any parts (body panels, etc).

RULE: f.8.8.2
            TITLE: ignore light bodywork, light nosecones, and outboard wheel assemblies

            CONTENT:
            rule F.8.8.2: Ignore light bodywork, light nosecones, and outboard wheel assemblies
F.8.8.2  
Ignore light bodywork, light nosecones, and outboard wheel assemblies
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 47 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: t.5.2.3
            TITLE: body panels or other existing covers are acceptable when constructed per t.5.2.7 / t.5.2.8

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.3: Body panels or other existing covers are acceptable when constructed per T.5.2.7 / T.5.2.8
T.5.2.3 Body panels or other existing covers are acceptable when constructed per T.5.2.7 / T.5.2.8

RULE: t.7.2.2
            TITLE: bodywork, a nose cone, or another component mounted to the vehicle is an aerodynamic

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.2.2: Bodywork, a nose cone, or another component mounted to the vehicle is an Aerodynamic
T.7.2.2 Bodywork, a nose cone, or another component mounted to the vehicle is an Aerodynamic 
Device if is designed to, or may possibly, produce force due to aerodynamic effects
